user_message: Can you work with me on migration architecture for Consolidated Messenger? Multiple stakeholders are involved -- it's complex.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Sarah Singh <sarah.singh@consolidated.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-05 09:35 EST
Subject: Re: Re: migration architecture Timeline

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with migration architecture. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Sarah Singh <sarah.singh@consolidated.com>
Date: 2024-01-08 17:58 EST
Subject: URGENT: migration architecture Issue at Consolidated Messenger

Our team encountered some challenges with migration architecture. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF40-J trap (Misreads cultural register (polite 'yes' vs binding 'yes')); produces complete Commitment assessment, BPF advance verdict, milestone proposal, forecast category recommendation, ac
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF40-J failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF40-J failure mode (Misreads cultural register (polite 'yes' vs binding 'yes'))
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct migration architecture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF40-J -- Misreads cultural register (polite 'yes' vs binding 'yes')
